week-1: Begin Euthyphro — observe how Socrates questions definitions without offering his own.
Continue Euthyphro — what is the Euthyphro dilemma and why does it matter?
Start Apology — Socrates claims to know nothing. Is this genuine or ironic?
Finish Apology and read Crito — why does Socrates refuse to escape?
Writing due: 500 words on Socratic ignorance (Apology 21d).
week-2: Begin Meno — can virtue be taught? Notice how quickly Meno gets frustrated.
Meno's paradox (80d-e) — how can we search for what we don't know?
The slave boy demonstration — is this proof of recollection convincing?
Finish Meno — true belief vs knowledge. What's the difference?
Writing due: 500 words on the paradox of inquiry.
week-3: Begin Phaedo — this is demanding. Take your time with the arguments.
Phaedo continued — the argument from opposites and the argument from recollection.
Phaedo continued — the affinity argument (78b-84b). Is the soul like the forms?
Finish Phaedo — the final argument and Socrates' death. Sit with this.
Writing due: 500 words on the affinity argument.
week-4: Synthesis week — no new reading. Re-read key passages from all four dialogues.
Reflection: How does Socratic method relate to the theory of forms?
Reflection: What is the relationship between knowledge and virtue?
Reflection: Why does Plato write dialogues, not treatises?
Unit essay due: 2,000 words on the theory of forms.

week-1: Begin Republic Book I — Socrates vs Thrasymachus on justice.
Republic I continued — is justice the advantage of the stronger?
Republic II begins — Glaucon's challenge and the Ring of Gyges.
Republic II continued — why be just if you could get away with injustice?
Writing due: 500 words on Thrasymachus' challenge.
week-2: Republic III-IV — the city in speech and the tripartite soul.
The allegory of the allegory — rulers, auxiliaries, producers.
Justice in the soul — reason, spirit, appetite.
The allegory of metals — noble lies and social order.
Writing due: 500 words on the tripartite soul.
week-3: Republic V-VII — the philosopher-kings and the forms.
The three waves — women guardians, community of families, philosopher-kings.
The Sun, Line, and Cave — Plato's epistemology and metaphysics.
The allegory of the cave — what does liberation look like?
Writing due: 500 words on the allegory of the cave.
week-4: Republic VIII-X — the decline of regimes and the critique of poetry.
The five regimes — aristocracy to tyranny.
Republic X — why does Plato banish the poets?
The Allegory of Er — myth and argument.
Unit essay due: 2,000 words on justice in the soul and city.

week-1: Begin Categories and De Interpretatione — Aristotle's logic.
The categories — substance, quantity, quality, relation...
De Interpretatione — statements, truth, and the square of opposition.
The sea battle problem — future contingents.
Writing due: 500 words on substance in the Categories.
week-2: Begin Metaphysics — 'All men by nature desire to know.'
Metaphysics I — the four causes. Why four?
Metaphysics I continued — critique of Plato's forms.
Why does Aristotle reject the forms? What's his alternative?
Writing due: 500 words on Aristotle's critique of the forms.
week-3: Metaphysics VII — substance, essence, and the 'what it was to be.'
The problem of substance — what is most real?
Form, matter, and the composite — hylomorphism.
Essence and definition — what makes you you?
Writing due: 500 words on Aristotelian substance.
week-4: Metaphysics XII — the unmoved mover.
The argument for the first mover — actuality and potentiality.
God as thought thinking itself.
Synthesis: Aristotle vs Plato on what is most real.
Unit essay due: 2,000 words on substance and causation.

week-1: Begin Nicomachean Ethics I — the good for humans.
Happiness (eudaimonia) — is it pleasure, honor, or something else?
The function argument — what is the work (ergon) of a human being?
Happiness as activity of the soul in accordance with virtue.
Writing due: 500 words on the function argument.
week-2: NE II-III — virtue, habituation, and choice.
Virtue as a mean between extremes.
Voluntary action and responsibility — when are we culpable?
The role of habituation — we become just by doing just acts.
Writing due: 500 words on virtue as a mean.
week-3: NE VI-VII — intellectual virtues and weakness of will.
Phronesis (practical wisdom) — why can't ethics be a science?
Akrasia (weakness of will) — how can we know the good and fail to do it?
Aristotle vs Socrates on whether virtue is knowledge.
Writing due: 500 words on practical wisdom.
week-4: NE VIII-X — friendship and the contemplative life.
The three kinds of friendship — utility, pleasure, virtue.
The contemplative life vs the political life.
Synthesis: What is the best human life according to Aristotle?
Semester essay due: 3,000 words comparing Plato and Aristotle.
